[[BLOCK:slide16_shape1]]
A hiker attempts to descend from the mountain summit at dusk. He surveys his surroundings, searching for all possible routes. As he proceeds along one path, he realizes that it does not lead him to his destination. He abandons this option because it would waste his time and energy, or the cost is too high. After several trials and errors, suboptimal choices are eliminated, and the hiker gradually moves in the direction of reduced losses. Ultimately, he selects the most cost-effective route, or in other words, a path that brings negative costs.
